title: feat: support AI-generated masks in shape_to_mask
body:
Summary:

This PR enables the export/import flow to handle AI-generated masks provided in JSON exports. Specifically:
- Mask data in shapes can now be provided as either base64-encoded images or as raw list/array formats (2D mask arrays).
- During loading (LabelFile.load), mask fields are decoded from multiple formats rather than assuming only base64 strings.
- During label-mapping (shapes_to_label), if a shape already carries a mask (AI-generated mask), that mask will be used directly to fill the label arrays instead of reconstructing the mask from polygon points.

What this enables:
- Seamless use of AI-generated masks in downstream workflows that rely on labelme’s mask-based shapes.
- Compatibility with both older labelme JSON exports (base64 masks) and new exports that include 2D mask arrays or list formats.

Testing guidance:
- Create a JSON export with a shape that includes a base64 mask and confirm it loads as a boolean mask and maps correctly to the label image.
- Create a JSON export with a shape that includes a 2D list/array mask and confirm it loads as a boolean mask and maps correctly.
- Create a JSON export with a shape that includes a base64 mask and verify that the shapes_to_label pipeline uses the mask directly when present.
- Validate that existing behavior (when no mask is provided) remains unchanged.

Impact:
- Small, targeted changes to two modules to support flexible AI-generated mask formats while preserving existing behavior for traditional masks.

step_plan.summary: Enable support for AI-generated masks in shape-to-mask workflow by accepting multiple mask formats on load and by using precomputed masks during shape-to-label mapping.

steps.operation: Extend LabelFile.load to decode masks from multiple formats and normalize to boolean numpy array
steps.reason: To support AI-generated masks provided as base64 strings or 2D list/array in JSON exports; ensure mask is stored as boolean mask for downstream label mapping
steps.side_effects: Downstream code consuming shapes.mask must handle boolean arrays; may require numpy availability; ensures backward compatibility with existing base64 mask data; may necessitate updates to unit tests that exercise mask decoding paths

steps.operation: Modify shapes_to_label to prefer using an existing precomputed 'mask' on a shape when present
steps.reason: Enable AI-generated masks to be consumed directly without reconstructing from polygon points; improves compatibility with JSON exports that include 2D masks
steps.side_effects: Label mapping now uses the provided mask directly when present; may require validation of mask dimensions and types; existing behavior remains for shapes without an inline mask; may require updates to tests and documentation
